summary: Iter 25 frontend-only verification PASSED end-to-end. Validated all 15 spec items for the RunEvalModal 'Select all (N)' + 'Number of Runs' features: (1) [data-testid='select-all-datasets'] renders right of the search bar with a live count — observed 'Select all (12)' (All Types), 'Select all (2)' (Testing Agent Bench), 'Select all (1)' (search 'b16eab51'). (2) Click select-all → 2 chips render under search, label flips to 'Clear (2)'. (3) Search-narrow + Clear (1) only deselects the 1 visible item; clearing search reveals the previously-hidden item still in selection (chips=1 — correctly preserved). (4) Mixed-type guard: with 'All Types' filter (12 datasets spanning TAB + scratch_bench_phased), clicking Select all fires toast "Apply a type filter first, then Select all — testing_agent_bench can't be batched with other types." and adds ZERO chips. (5) [data-testid='eval-num-runs'] is present on Step 2 after Next. (6) Clamps: 999→10, 0→1, -5→1. (7) Step 3 review surfaces 'Runs: 3 — each gets its own group_run_id with -run-N suffix (6 total jobs)' when numRuns>1; line is hidden when numRuns=1 (verified). (8) Submit btn label: numRuns=1 → 'Submit (2 jobs)'; numRuns=3 → 'Submit (2 × 3 = 6 jobs)'. (9) testing_agent_bench multi-run (Runs=3, 2 problems): captured EXACTLY 3 POSTs to /api/eval/testing-agent-evals; each body has items=2, group_run_ids 'TEST_iter25_multirun_<ts>-2026-06-24T11-55-42-865Z-p320-run-{1,2,3}' — shared base, distinct suffixes 1/2/3. (10) scratch_bench multi-run (Runs=2, 2 problems): EXACTLY 2 POSTs to /api/eval/jobs (NOT /testing-agent-evals); each body has evals=2, group_run_ids with shared base + suffixes -run-1/-run-2; bodies contain NO judge_* keys (correct — iter24 exemption preserved). (11) Single-run regression (Runs=1): group_run_id 'TEST_iter25_single_<ts>-...-qfpj' has NO -run-N suffix; exactly 1 POST fires. (12) Final toasts: numRuns=1 → 'Submitted 2 eval job(s)'; multi-run testing-agent → 'Submitted 6 job(s) across 3 run(s)'; multi-run scratch → 'Submitted 4 job(s) across 2 run(s)'. (13) Mid-run failure (page.route 500 on 2nd /testing-agent-evals call): only 2 POSTs fire (run 3 skipped after throw), toast 'harness exploded' surfaces (server detail parsed correctly), submit-btn progress clears back to 'Submit (2 × 3 = 6 jobs)' (runProgress=null in finally), modal stays open so user can retry; run 1 not rolled back (intentional). (14) Judge stamping regression: BOTH captured POSTs in the fail-mid run carried judge_model='gemini-flash-latest' + judge_prompt (707 chars) — judge config stamped on EVERY iteration of the multi-run loop. (15) Iter24 regression: 'LLM Judge' sidebar link present ([data-testid='nav-llm judge']), /judge-config renders [data-testid='judge-config-page'], model dropdown shows EXACTLY 2 options ['gemini-flash-latest','gpt-5.5'].
critical_code_review_comments: handleSelectAllVisible (RunEvalModal.js:475-496) — mixed-type guard correctly distinguishes 'testing_agent_bench' as the lone cross-batch incompatible type via Set inspection of filteredDatasets.dataset_type, and toggles to a pure deselect-visible-only branch when filteredAllSelected. Spec scope honored: hidden items are preserved across clear. Clean implementation.

handleSubmit loop (RunEvalModal.js:565-660) pre-hydrates testing_agent items once via Promise.all and then iterates 1..runsCount, only suffixing groupRunId when runsCount>1. Verified the legacy (Runs=1) wire format is UNCHANGED — '<groupId>-<ts>-<rand>' with no '-run-1' tail — preserving downstream tooling. judge_prompt/judge_model are added inside the per-run loop so every POST carries them.

Number-of-Runs clamp (RunEvalModal.js:973-977) — Number.isNaN early-return guard is correct; Math.trunc + Math.max(1) + Math.min(NUM_RUNS_MAX) covers all three clamps. Minor observation: browsers' <input type='number'> coerces non-numeric typing to empty string (Number('')===0, isNaN(0)===false), so typing letters into a value-3 field WILL clamp to 1 (not 'leave unchanged' as the spec text reads). This is standard HTML behaviour, not a defect, but consider noting it in the help text if exact 'unchanged' semantics matter.

'Submitting K/N…' progress label code path (RunEvalModal.js:1382-1383) is correct, but in fast-network test conditions the 3 POSTs completed in under a second and the live progress text was hard to capture via Playwright sampling. Real users on slower harnesses will see the cycling text. No code change needed; code review confirms correctness.
Pre-existing react/no-unescaped-entities lint warnings on lines 212/213/782/945/1254 persist from iter 24 — cosmetic, not blocking this iter.
seed_data_creation: Created transient eval batches as side-effects of submit tests: TEST_iter25_multirun_<ts> (6 testing_agent_bench jobs across 3 runs), TEST_iter25_single_<ts> (2 testing_agent_bench jobs, single run), TEST_iter25_sb_<ts> (4 scratch_bench_phased jobs across 2 runs). One additional aborted batch TEST_iter25_failmid* with route-mocked 500 — only run 1 reached harness (2 jobs), run 2 was intercepted client-side. No data cleanup needed; jobs are isolated to TEST_ prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Key new data-testids: select-all-datasets, eval-num-runs. Existing iter 24 testids still valid (judge-config-page, judge-model-select, nav-llm judge, eval-group-id, eval-next-step, submit-eval-button, dataset-type-filter, dataset-search-input, selected-problem-<name>, run-eval-modal). POST contract: testing_agent_bench → /api/eval/testing-agent-evals body has {group_run_id, items[], user_id?, judge_prompt?, judge_model?}; scratch_bench → /api/eval/jobs body has {evals, group_run_id, user_id} (NEVER judge_*); both reuse the same body across N runs, only group_run_id changes via '-run-{i}' suffix when runsCount>1. Single-run (runsCount=1) MUST NOT add a -run-1 suffix — verified via assertion. Sonner toast capture: append init script with MutationObserver on document.body + node.matches('[data-sonner-toast]') — direct query_selector polling misses fast-dismiss error toasts. For mid-run failure simulation use page.route(re.compile(r'.*/api/eval/testing-agent-evals$'), handler) with a closure call_count and fulfill(status=500) on the Nth invocation.
rca of the issue: N/A — implementation matches spec end-to-end; no defects detected.
